definition: An order issued by an appellate court (in this case, the Supreme Court) directing a lower court to send a case for review. The Supreme Court grants certiorari only when it believes a case involves important legal questions that warrant its review, typically accepting fewer than 100 cases out of over 7,000 petitions annually.
examples: Jules filed a petition for certiorari with the Supreme Court after the Second Circuit's decision, and the Court granted his petition on December 5, 2025
